message:
fix(messaging/redis): PubSub ping preserves interleaved subscription messages instead of dropping them

RedisPubSub::ping() previously called next_event() in a loop
discarding any Message or Subscription events while waiting for
PONG.  This silently dropped real messages that arrived between
the PING command and its response.

Fix: ping() now calls the internal read_next_event() and buffers
interleaved non-PONG events into a pending_events VecDeque.
next_event() drains pending_events before reading from the wire,
ensuring no messages are lost.  reset() clears pending_events
along with subscriptions.

New helper read_next_event() extracted from the former next_event()
implementation for internal use by ping().

Tests:
- pubsub_ping_preserves_interleaved_messages: mock server injects
  a subscription message between PING and PONG, verifies the
  message is returned by next_event() after ping completes.
- Additional test infrastructure: read_resp_frame helper,
  assert_completes_within, run_test_with_cx integration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
